headline: McDonald’s (MCD) Brings Back Fried Apple Pie After More Than 30 Years
summary: McDonald's is bringing back its Fried Apple Pie for a limited time starting June 22. The dessert was removed from U.S. menus in 1992 and has been a frequent subject of customer requests. The move is part of a nostalgia focused marketing push aimed at boosting engagement and store visits. For investors watching McDonald's, the return of the Fried Apple Pie adds a consumer focused angle to a stock that last closed at $287.93 on the NYSE:MCD ticker. Over the past 5 years, the stock is up...

headline: McDonald’s and Cava Are Winning. Diners Are Going Value or Premium.
summary: Restaurant spending is fragmenting as consumers trade down for value or up for experiences, leaving midtier brands struggling.

headline: Will Yum! Brands' $2.7 Billion Pizza Hut Sale Unlock More Value?
summary: YUM is selling Pizza Hut in a $2.7B deal, aiming to streamline operations, boost flexibility and return more capital to shareholders.

headline: McDonald's revives beloved menu item after 34 years
summary: When you think of quintessential American foods, a few things immediately come to mind: fried chicken, saucy barbecue, hamburgers, and apple pie. Apple pie may be one of the nation’s most iconic desserts, but it wasn’t actually invented here. The first known recipe dates back to England ...
